remaining_tasks.name: Fix cursor VISIBLE BUT SOLID after window resize
Retest split pane cursor freeze (not yet verified)

blockers.description: Cursor visible but not blinking after resize. Timer runs (wakeup_cb #61 fires). Root cause hypothesis: renderer Thread.zig renderCallback calls updateFrame(cursor_blink_visible) then drawFrame(now=false). With must_draw_from_app_thread=true, drawFrame checks hasVsync() - if TRUE, early-returns WITHOUT pushing redraw_surface. App-side queue_render presents LAST BAKED frame with stale cursor state.
blockers.workaround: NEXT: Read ghostty/src/renderer/generic.zig lines 1033-1050 hasVsync() impl. If true for embedded Linux OpenGL, need to disable vsync OR use drawNow signal path for cursor blink.

decisions.decision: Removed false/true focus bounce from both restore_active_pane_focus and connect_resize
decisions.rationale: Bounce permanently kills cursor timer via async cancel race: set_focus(false) initiates cancel (cursor_c_cancel). set_focus(true) arrives before cancel fires - cursor_c.state()==.active - restart skipped - cancel fires - timer dead forever. See Thread.zig focused=true handler line 407.

next_action: Read ghostty/src/renderer/generic.zig lines 1033-1050 to check hasVsync() for embedded renderer. If hasVsync=true, fix is to either disable vsync in embedded config or ensure draw_now.notify() is called after cursor blink state change.
context_notes: User confirmed cursor is VISIBLE BUT SOLID (not blinking) after resize. Wakeup_cb fires, renders happen. Ghostty bakes frame in renderCallback via updateFrame(cursor_blink_visible). If hasVsync=true, drawFrame(false) skips app notification. cmux wakeup_cb calls queue_render directly but presents stale baked frame.
